# Plan — 2026-05-29

## Inputs

- Research file: `.github/research/2026-05-29-research.md`
- 4 of 5 items from 2026-05-19 already shipped (MCP pagination, startup timeout, rewind, prompt-history dedup).
- The two "still open" items from 2026-05-19 are also already implemented in this codebase:
  - `/goal` autonomous loop: `src/command/goal.ts` + `src/cli/interactive.ts:751` + `src/cli/tui/hooks/useCommands.ts:358`.
  - Per-`execute()` agent usage reset is N/A — our `Agent` (`src/agent/index.ts`) has no `execute()` that accumulates usage; `agenticChat.ts` re-creates state per call.
- This plan therefore focuses on the **5 new gaps** identified in the 2026-05-29 research.

## Issues created

| # | Issue | Title | Category | Size |
|---|-------|-------|----------|------|
| 1 | [#503](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/503) | [provider] Automatic `--fallback-model` switch when primary model is missing | provider | small (< 4h) |
| 2 | [#507](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/507) | [tool] Read tool PARTIAL-view fallback for oversized files | tool | small (< 4h) |
| 3 | [#505](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/505) | [command] `sessions --json` for scriptable session metadata | command | small (< 4h) |
| 4 | [#504](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/504) | [skill] Honor `disallowed-tools` frontmatter for skills and slash commands | skill | small (< 4h) |
| 5 | [#506](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/506) | [command] `/code-review` slash command with selectable effort level | command | medium (1-2 days) |

Total: 5 issues (matches research recommendation order: smallest reliability win first, medium command last).

## Per-issue plans

### 1. `--fallback-model` automatic provider switch (provider, small)

**Files to modify**

- `src/providers/index.ts` — add `getProviderForModelWithFallback(modelId, options?)` that wraps `getProviderForModel`, validates the model id against `ORCHESTRATION_MODELS`/`isOrchestrationModel`, and on rejection re-tries with `routingConfig.preferences.fallbackModel`. Emit a single `console.warn` (or bus event) `"Primary model X not found, falling back to Y"`.
- `src/core/orchestrator.ts` and `src/core/agenticChat.ts` — call the new helper instead of `getProviderForModel` directly.
- Optionally: extend `RoutingPreferences` in `src/config/routingConfig.ts` to also support a process-level `--fallback-model` CLI flag (read in `src/cli/program.ts`), exposed via env `ALEXI_FALLBACK_MODEL` for non-interactive use.

**Tests to write**

- `src/providers/index.test.ts` — mock `isOrchestrationModel` to return false for a typo'd id, assert the helper returns a provider built for `fallbackModel` and emits the warning **once** per session (not per call).
- Regression test: when the primary model is valid, no fallback is consulted and no warning fires.

**Approach**

- Use a module-level `Set<string>` to dedup warnings per process, mirroring how `connectivity.ts` handles single-shot startup messages.
- Keep the change additive — `getProviderForModel` keeps current throwing behavior; existing callers opt in.

**Done when**

- `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test` green.
- A typo'd `AICORE_MODEL` no longer breaks every chat turn for the rest of the session.

---

### 2. Read tool PARTIAL-view fallback (tool, small)

**Files to modify**

- `src/tool/tools/read.ts` — after `truncateOutput()` returns `wasTruncated=true`, in the no-`offset`/no-`limit` case, replace the current `hint` with a `"PARTIAL view — file has N lines, showing 1..K. Call read again with offset=K+1 to continue."` string. Set a new field `partial: true` on the result so the model knows this is a paginated read, not a hard error.
- `src/tool/index.ts` — extend `ToolResult<T>` (or the read-specific result type) to include optional `partial: boolean`.

**Tests to write**

- `src/tool/tools/__tests__/read.test.ts` (or add file) — write a temp file with > MAX_LINES lines, call `readTool.execute({ filePath })` with no offset/limit, assert `data.partial === true`, `truncated === true`, and the hint text mentions "offset=" with the correct continuation line.
- Regression test: small file returns no `partial`, no `truncated`.

**Approach**

- Reuse the existing `truncateOutput` + `endIdx` math; only the wording and the new flag change.
- Keep the streaming path in `readFileStreaming` untouched.

**Done when**

- `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test` green.
- Big-file reads return a usable first page with continuation guidance instead of a silently-truncated string.

---

### 3. `sessions --json` for scriptable session metadata (command, small)

**Files to modify**

- `src/cli/commands/sessions.ts` — add `.option('--json', 'Output sessions as JSON array')` to the `sessions` subcommand. When `opts.json` is true, `console.log(JSON.stringify(sessions.map(s => ({ id, title, model: s.modelId ?? null, updatedAt: s.updated, messageCount: s.messageCount, totalTokens: s.totalTokens })), null, 2))` and skip the human header.
- (Optional) Add the same `--json` flag to `agent` listing in `src/cli/commands/agent.ts` if/when that subcommand exists; out of scope if it doesn't.

**Tests to write**

- `src/cli/commands/__tests__/sessions.test.ts` — mock `SessionManager.listSessions()`, run the command with `--json`, capture stdout, parse it as JSON, assert shape `[{ id, title, model, updatedAt, messageCount, totalTokens }, ...]`.
- Regression test: without `--json`, the human format still prints `=== Saved Sessions ===` header.

**Approach**

- Follow the existing `session-export -o` pattern — Commander option, branch on opts in the action handler.
- Keep the JSON shape stable and documented in the command help text so external tools (status bars, tmux-resurrect-style tools) can rely on it.

**Done when**

- `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test` green.
- `alexi sessions --json | jq '.[].id'` works on a populated session store.

---

### 4. Honor `disallowed-tools` frontmatter for skills and slash commands (skill, small)

**Files to modify**

- `src/skill/index.ts` — `SkillSchema` already has `disabledTools: z.array(z.string()).optional()`. Add an alias-accepting parser in `loadSkillFromFile` that ALSO reads `data['disallowed-tools']` and `data.disallowedTools` from frontmatter (kebab-case is the upstream Claude Code convention). Same for `tools` (already present).
- `src/command/index.ts` (and any markdown-command loader) — wire the same field for slash commands so a `.md` command with `disallowed-tools: [bash, write]` removes those tools from the registry list for that turn.
- `src/core/agenticChat.ts:357` — when an active **skill** (not just an agent) is in scope for the current turn, intersect `enabledToolNames` with `(allTools - skill.disabledTools)`. Today only the agent's `canUseTool` is consulted.
- `src/tool/tools/skill.ts` — when the `skill` tool is invoked, surface the skill's `disabledTools` back to the orchestrator so it can filter for the **next** turn (not just include it in the result).

**Tests to write**

- `src/skill/index.test.ts` — load a fixture markdown file with `disallowed-tools: [bash, write]` frontmatter, assert the parsed skill has `disabledTools: ['bash', 'write']`.
- `src/core/__tests__/agenticChat.test.ts` (or add file) — mock a skill with `disabledTools: ['bash']` as the active skill, assert `bash` is not in the formatted tool list passed to the provider.
- Regression test: skills without the field still see the full tool list.

**Approach**

- Treat `disallowed-tools` (kebab-case, upstream parity) and `disabledTools` (camel-case, our existing field) as synonyms in the loader.
- Only apply per-turn filtering — do NOT mutate the global registry.

**Done when**

- `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test` green.
- A skill markdown with `disallowed-tools: [bash]` cannot trigger bash tool calls while active.

---

### 5. `/code-review` slash command (command, medium)

**Files to create / modify**

- `src/command/codeReview.ts` (new) — exports `executeCodeReview(options)` similar in shape to `executeGoal`. Accepts:
  - `effort: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` (default `medium`) — maps to a system-prompt instruction and a routing hint (`high` → reasoning model from `routingConfig.models`).
  - `target: 'uncommitted' | 'branch' | { base: string }` — selects the diff source via `git diff` (uncommitted = `git diff HEAD`, branch = `git diff <base>...HEAD`).
  - `fix?: boolean` — when true, run a second pass that applies safe simplification edits via the existing `edit` tool (gated behind permission `auto-edit-on-review`).
  - `comment?: { repo, prNumber }` — when set, post findings as inline comments via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{N}/comments` (use `bash` tool with the existing GitHub allowlist).
- `src/cli/interactive.ts` — wire `/code-review` slash command analogous to the `goal` block at line 751 (parse args, call `executeCodeReview`, render result with the same color helpers).
- `src/cli/tui/hooks/useCommands.ts` — add `/code-review` entry mirroring the `goal` registration at line 358.
- `src/cli/utils/completer.ts` — add `{ name: 'code-review', description: '...', category: 'general' }`.
- Reuse `src/skill/skills/index.ts` `codeReviewSkill.prompt` as the base system prompt, with effort-specific extensions appended.
- Optionally: a thin CLI subcommand `alexi code-review` in `src/cli/commands/codeReview.ts` for non-interactive use (CI integration).

**Tests to write**

- `src/command/codeReview.test.ts` — unit-test `executeCodeReview` with mocked `agenticChat` and a fake `git diff` source. Cover: low/medium/high effort prompt differences; uncommitted vs branch diff selection; `--fix` triggers an `edit` tool call only after the review pass.
- `src/cli/commands/__tests__/codeReview.test.ts` (if CLI subcommand added) — Commander wiring smoke test.
- Mock `bash` tool for `--comment` so tests don't shell out to `gh`.

**Approach**

- Split implementation into two PRs:
  - **PR A (this issue):** the command + diff selection + effort plumbing + reuse of the `code-review` skill prompt. No `--fix`, no `--comment`.
  - **PR B (follow-up, not in this batch):** `--fix` apply-mode + `--comment` GitHub integration. Track as a separate auto-implement issue once PR A merges.
- Rationale: keeps the "medium 1-2 day" estimate honest and mirrors how upstream Claude Code split `/code-review --fix` from the base review.

**Done when**

- `/code-review` runs inside the interactive TUI and prints structured MUST FIX / SHOULD IMPROVE / NICE TO HAVE sections from the model, populated from real `git diff` output of the current branch.
- `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test` green.

## Sequencing recommendation

1. Issue 1 (provider fallback) — smallest, biggest reliability win, unblocks everything else if model ids drift.
2. Issues 2 + 3 + 4 in parallel — all small, no overlap.
3. Issue 5 (medium) last in this cycle.

## Out of scope (deferred per research)

- Opus 4.8 / dynamic workflows / `/workflows` — upstream model rollout, outside SAP-AI-Core envelope.
- `MessageDisplay` hook — not justified yet, re-evaluate next cycle.
